title: GameCubeGamepadMapping
description: Mapping for Nintendo GameCube controllers (typically connected via a USB adapter such as the official Nintendo adapter for Wii U / Switch). Inherits the full GenericDualAnalogGamepadMapping button and axis layout. Note that the GameCube controller has no right-stick click, no D-pad as discrete buttons on all adapters, and uses analog shoulders — the exact channel availability depends on the adapter's HID report.

sections.title: Import
paragraphs: Mapping for Nintendo GameCube controllers (typically connected via a USB adapter such as the official Nintendo adapter for Wii U / Switch).

Inherits the full GenericDualAnalogGamepadMapping button and axis layout. Note that the GameCube controller has no right-stick click, no D-pad as discrete buttons on all adapters, and uses analog shoulders — the exact channel availability depends on the adapter's HID report.

members.name: destroy
members.description: Releases all button and axis control references held by this mapping. Call when the associated gamepad disconnects to allow garbage collection.

members.name: hasChannel
members.description: Returns true when this mapping declares at least one button or axis control that writes to channel. Use to detect device-specific capabilities at runtime — e.g. before binding an input to a right-stick channel that may not exist on a single Joy-Con.

members.name: axes
members.description: Ordered list of axes, indexed by the Gamepad API axis index.

members.name: buttons
members.description: Ordered list of buttons, indexed by the Gamepad API button index.

members.name: family
members.description: Identifies the device family this mapping targets.
